message: So there I was at my in-laws' place last week when the power went out — a full neighborhood blackout, no eta. And honestly? For once I wasn't the person scrambling around asking "do you have any flashlights?" 😅 I actually *had* my lantern in the car, a couple of power banks charged up, and a basic idea of where the food situation stood. My MIL was genuinely impressed, which felt weird in the best way. Turns out all those little prep habits actually stick, and they *do* make a difference when things go sideways. Wasn't about being a doomsday person — just about not wanting to feel helpless in my own evening. The peace of mind? Totally worth it. Have you got a "prepared moment" that surprised you, or is blackout season still catching you off guard?
